question: How to create an accordion without JavaScript
description: Use the native <details> and <summary> elements to build fully functional, accessible accordions with zero JavaScript and no dependencies.

steps.title: Basic <details> and <summary>
steps.explanation: <details> is a disclosure widget. Clicking <summary> toggles the open state of all sibling content. The open attribute marks it expanded by default.
steps.code:
What is HTML?

HTML (HyperText Markup Language) is the standard markup language used to structure content on the web.

What is CSS?

CSS (Cascading Style Sheets) controls the visual presentation of HTML elements.

steps.title: Style the marker and animate the open state
steps.explanation: Remove the default triangle with list-style: none on summary. Use CSS grid or max-height to animate the expanding content — no JavaScript needed.

steps.title: Build a full accordion stack
steps.explanation: Stack multiple <details> elements inside a wrapper for a classic accordion. They operate independently by default — any number can be open simultaneously.

steps.title: Exclusive accordion — only one item open at a time
steps.explanation: HTML 2024 introduced the name attribute on <details>. All <details> elements sharing the same name behave like a radio group — opening one closes the others automatically.
